name: Rice Pudding Pear Tart
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare pie crust according to package directions. Place in a 10-inch tart pan. Bake at 450°F for 8 to 10 minutes or until lightly browned; set aside.
2. In a 10-inch skillet, combine wine and cinnamon. Bring to a boil. Add pears; reduce heat, cover and poach for 10 minutes. Turn pears in liquid and continue to poach for an additional 5 to 10 minutes or until pears are tender. Remove from wine; set aside.
3. In a 3-quart saucepan, combine rice, half-and-half, sugar, butter, and salt. Cook over medium heat for 12 to 15 minutes or until slightly thickened.
4. Gradually stir one-fourth of the hot pudding mixture into eggs; return egg mixture to saucepan, stirring constantly. Continue to cook for 1 to 2 minutes. Stir in vanilla extract.
5. Pour pudding mixture into the pre-baked crust. Place pears, cut-side down, on cutting surface. Cut thin lengthwise slits in each pear, one-third down from stem end. Fan pears over pudding mixture.
6. Reduce oven temperature to 350°F. Bake for 30 minutes or until pudding is set. Remove from oven; sprinkle with an additional tablespoon of sugar.
7. Place tart in the oven about 4 to 5 inches from the heat source. Broil for 1 to 2 minutes or until top is browned. Cool before serving. Tart can be made ahead, if desired.
